questions.q: What are the five layers of a small online business in 2026?
choices: Website, social media, ads, SEO, content
questions.explain: Landing, product, email, monetization, analytics — each with its own automation profile.

questions.q: Short answer: which layer has the highest leverage in a small online business, and why?
questions.explain: Email. Every other layer funnels back into the list — sign-ups, customers, monetisation, and the analytics that tell you what to email next. Pick one layer and justify it.

questions.q: Which of these should you automate FIRST?
questions.explain: The welcome sequence is in DO FIRST: cheap to build, high recurring payoff, foundation for everything else. CRM and chatbot are AVOID; LinkedIn is SKIP.

questions.q: You're building a niche directory. Which layer should Claude Code own, and which should Hermes own?
choices: Claude Code: directory listings scraping. Hermes: landing page copy.
Claude Code: directory MVP build. Hermes: weekly digest + analytics cron.
Claude Code: Stripe webhooks. Hermes: payment receipts.
Both harnesses should do the same work — pick one.
questions.explain: Claude Code ships the directory MVP (Layer 2 — work lives in a repo, needs test verification). Hermes runs the weekly digest cron and the analytics alert (Layers 3 and 5 — long-running, outside a codebase).

questions.q: Per the source videos, what does the '$3/month business' headline actually refer to?
choices: Total business cost including models and time
questions.explain: Infrastructure only — Hostinger VPS + domain. Realistic total cash is $15–$35/mo once you add email, analytics, and model API costs. Time is the biggest cost of all.

questions.q: Short answer: name ONE thing from the SKIP or AVOID quadrant and explain why it doesn't belong in a week-one stack.
questions.explain: Free response. Good answers: a custom CRM (buy, don't build), a chatbot for support (high effort, low payoff — a welcome email usually solves it), daily LinkedIn posts (audience isn't yours), or any AI wrapper demo (no defensibility).
